description: Export an audited Locus plugin archive from existing Skill packages, Views, and optional Rules. Use this only after the /plugin workflow has inspected the selected components, planned the plugin directory/manifest structure, classified project dependencies, and received explicit user approval for the export path. The tool requires auditSummary, structurePlan, and userApproval so the tool call records the analysis that justified packaging.

id.description: Plugin id chosen by the user, for example asset-tools or unity-asset-browser. Prefer concise package-manager-style ids; use namespacing only when the user confirms it or a collision needs disambiguation.

version.description: Plugin version written to locus.plugin.json, for example 0.1.0.

filePath.description: Absolute or workspace-resolved .zip output path approved by the user.

skillPackageIds.description: Existing Skill package ids to copy into skills/<package-id>. Markdown-only Skills are not valid here.

viewIds.description: Existing View ids to copy into views/<view-id>.

ruleFiles.description: Optional plugin Rules to write into rules/<file-name>.md and declare under components.rules. These Rules are installed as read-only optional rules and can be enabled per Agent in the Agent view.

fileName.description: Rule file name, with or without .md. Use lowercase letters, digits, hyphens, underscores, or dots.

content.description: Markdown content for the optional Rule.

projectDependencies.description: Project dependencies that make this plugin project-dependent. Leave empty only after auditing that the plugin is project-independent.

kind.description: Dependency kind, such as unityPackage, assembly, script, asset, or custom.

name.description: Concrete dependency name, type name, assembly name, package id, asset path, or script path.

notes.description: Optional short note explaining why the dependency is required.

installAfterExport.description: Install the exported plugin into the local Locus plugin directory after the archive is created. In the /plugin workflow this defaults to true.

installScope.description: Local install scope used when installAfterExport is true. Use app for user-wide plugins and project for the current workspace.

transferOwnership.description: After a successful local install, remove the original exported Skill/View packages so the installed plugin becomes their owner. Rule files supplied through ruleFiles are created directly in the plugin archive. In the /plugin workflow this defaults to true and requires installAfterExport.

auditSummary.description: Required summary of inspected files, completeness checks, project-dependency classification, plugin-managed source checks, and remaining risks.

structurePlan.description: Required planned plugin structure and manifest metadata, including components.skills/components.views/components.rules layout and dependency metadata.

userApproval.description: Required user approval or direct user instruction authorizing this exact export after the audit and structure plan were presented.
